description: Martial damage from weapons, creatures, and raw force. Resisted by Physiology (Hardening). No magical origin.

types.name: Blunt
types.description: Crushing impact from hammers, clubs, falls, and slams.
types.interaction_logic: Most effective against brittle bone structures and crystalline creatures. Ignores thin armor but stopped by plate and stone. Deals extra damage when target is prone or stunned.
types.status_trigger: Stagger (chance to lose next initiative)
types.special_note: Stone Folk have exceptional innate resistance to Blunt damage.

types.name: Slashing
types.description: Cutting force from swords, axes, claws, and bladed weapons.
types.interaction_logic: Effective against lightly armored or unarmored targets and plant-based creatures. Causes bleed on living targets. Reduced by heavy armor.
types.status_trigger: Bleed (lingering damage per turn, living targets only)
types.special_note: Oakpeople and plant creatures are vulnerable to Slashing.

types.name: Piercing
types.description: Puncturing force from arrows, spears, bites, and thrusting blades.
types.interaction_logic: Bypasses the surface of most armor types but stopped by layered materials. Most consistent physical type with few immunity exceptions.
types.status_trigger: Wound (reduces Metabolism by 5% per stack)
types.special_note: Plated-Reptilian creatures have exceptional innate resistance to Piercing.

types.name: Rupturing
types.description: Internal tearing damage from hydraulic pressure, explosions, or rupture-type weapons. Bypasses standard armor.
types.interaction_logic: Ignores standard Hardening to a degree; acts on internal Vitality directly. Very rare as a weapon type. Often triggered as a secondary effect of other damage.
types.status_trigger: Internal Rupture (percentage of max HP lost over next 3 turns)
types.special_note: Rare. Mostly found in Abyssal creatures and high-tier magical effects.

description: Toxins, infections, and organic hazards. Resisted by Physiology (Immunity / Hardening-Biological). Not magical in nature.

types.name: Venomous
types.description: Injected biological toxins from bites, stings, or envenomed weapons.
types.interaction_logic: Must pierce the skin to take effect. Has no effect on undead, constructs, or creatures without biological systems. Degrades Metabolism and Vigor.
types.status_trigger: Envenomed (Metabolism and Vigor reduced; stacks up to 3 times)
types.special_note: Swampbrood and many Boglands creatures are immune. Oakpeople and Wildmen have partial resistance.

types.name: Poisonous
types.description: Ingested or inhaled toxins from contaminated food, gas, or alchemical compounds.
types.interaction_logic: Can affect targets through ingestion or proximity (gas clouds). Has no effect on undead, constructs, or plant creatures.
types.status_trigger: Poisoned (Cognition rolls suffer penalty; Vitality degrades slowly)
types.special_note: Swampbrood are largely immune. Ashen Halfbreeds have moderate resistance.

description: Environmental hazard damage tied to biome conditions. Primarily affects long-term survival on the world map. Rarely lethal in combat but degrades Conviction in mismatched environments.

types.name: Hot
types.description: Extreme heat from desert environments, volcanic regions, or sustained fire exposure.
types.interaction_logic: Primarily degrades Metabolism (Stamina) and Conviction on the world map. Ice Archons are severely vulnerable; Wildmen and Common Folk have natural weathering tolerance.
types.status_trigger: Heatstroke (Metabolism -10%; Conviction -5 per biome turn in unprotected heat)

types.name: Cold
types.description: Extreme cold from arctic tundra, frozen mountain passes, or supernatural chill effects.
types.interaction_logic: Degrades Metabolism and Conviction on the world map. Ice Archons and Wildmen are immune. Most others suffer Conviction penalties in The Eternal Winds unless equipped for cold.
types.status_trigger: Frostbitten (Metabolism -15%; Conviction loss accelerated)
types.special_note: Ice Archons are completely immune. Wildmen have high natural resistance.

types.name: Humidity
types.description: Oppressive moisture and rot-inducing damp from swamp, jungle, or bog environments.
types.interaction_logic: Degrades equipment durability and causes Blight vulnerability over time. Also slowly reduces Vitality for characters without immunity.
types.status_trigger: Waterlogged (Blight vulnerability +10%; equipment degrades 1 tier per 5 biome turns)

description: Magical damage derived from faith, conviction, and the pure light of the materium. Common with Warden, Soulkindler, and holy archetypes. Especially effective against undead and dark arts users.

types.name: Purelight
types.description: Raw, searing brilliance. The base damage type of Lightwielding. Burns with divine clarity.
types.interaction_logic: Especially effective against undead and creatures with high Shadow Connection. Deals bonus damage to characters with low Conviction. Partially resisted by full plate armor (reflects some light).
types.status_trigger: Seared (Undead: loses turn; Living dark-arts users: Conviction drain)
types.special_note: The Destroyer birth sign characters suffer extra vulnerability to Purelight.

types.name: Solacium
types.description: A non-lethal force that forcibly calms the target, draining Tenacity and initiative.
types.interaction_logic: Does not reduce HP directly. Drains Tenacity (sub-trait of Conviction) and resets initiative order. Cannot kill. Extremely effective on high-Conviction enemies who rely on initiative.
types.status_trigger: Pacified (next initiative lost; Tenacity -10 for 2 turns)
types.resisted_by: Conviction (Tenacity); The Stoic birth sign is immune
types.special_note: The Stoic birth sign characters are completely immune to Solacium.

types.name: Aegis Reflection
types.description: Concussive hard-light force reflected from barriers or shields. Found in auras and rare objects.
types.interaction_logic: Deals area-of-effect light damage emanating from a barrier. Can be emitted passively by rare enchanted items. Also found naturally in certain sacred locations.
types.status_trigger: Knockback (pushed 1 hex) + Blinded (1 turn)
types.special_note: The Guardian birth sign adds bonus Aegis Reflection to team members.

types.name: Verdicium
types.description: A psychic 'truth' strike that deals damage scaled to the target's hidden Scarring or guilt. Hurts more the weaker the Conviction.
types.interaction_logic: Damage scales inversely with target Conviction. Against a character with Conviction 80, minimal damage. Against Conviction 10, catastrophic. Bypasses physical armor entirely.
types.status_trigger: Exposed (Scarring sub-trait visible to caster's team for 3 turns)
types.special_note: Completely ineffective against The Stoic birth sign characters.

types.name: Blessed Materium
types.description: Found only in rare sacred items. Bypasses all physical resistances and deals physical damage equivalent to the weapon's type, but with a holy quality.
types.interaction_logic: Treats all physical resistance as zero. Does not deal magical damage — the damage type is still physical, just blessed. Very rare.
types.status_trigger: Holy Wound (cannot be regenerated by dark arts healing for 2 turns)
types.special_note: Only found in artifacts. Cannot be crafted by most Artisans.

description: Elemental and natural force damage derived from the physical laws of the world. The most common magical damage category. Used by elementalists, shamans, and witches. Deep synergies between types.

types.name: Impact
types.description: Pure, invisible physical force. The base damage type of the Materium school. The 'universal language of moving matter.'
types.interaction_logic: Virtually no creatures are immune. Deals consistent damage with no secondary effects. The 'safe' choice when element is unknown. Works on incorporeal beings (unlike mundane blunt).
types.status_trigger: Concussed (Physiology rolls -5% for 1 turn)
types.special_note: Every practitioner of Materium learns Impact first.

types.name: Earthen
types.description: Vibrational earth energy that shatters bone and stone. Ignores light armor.
types.interaction_logic: Bypasses leather and padded armor (ignores Hardening from light armor). Deals bonus damage to Stone Folk and stone/earth-type creatures despite their high resistances. Extreme forms generate earthquakes or summon earth constructs.
types.status_trigger: Buried (target cannot move next turn on a critical roll)
types.resisted_by: Stone Folk have innate partial resistance; Physiology (Hardening) high-tier
types.special_note: Deep synergy with Temporal (causes accelerated stone crumbling).

types.name: Tempest
types.description: High-pressure wind and atmospheric cutting force. Invisible and silent. Also generates electric rupture with cascade effects.
types.interaction_logic: Cascade: every Tempest hit has a 15% chance to generate an electric secondary strike on an adjacent target. This secondary strike also carries the cascade chance. Invisible — perception roll needed to detect origin.
types.status_trigger: Shocked (cascade electric charge; cannot use reaction abilities next turn)
types.synergy: Aquas (combo triggers Tempest-surge, +20% Tempest on soaked targets)

types.name: Fire
types.description: Thermal heat that melts, burns, and shapes. Simple but devastatingly effective at scale.
types.interaction_logic: Lingering burn effect on living targets. Exceptional against plant life and ice-type creatures. Wildmen resist fire better than most. Ice Archons are severely vulnerable.
types.status_trigger: Ignited (1d4+caster_level fire damage per turn until extinguished or 3 turns pass)
types.synergy: Earthen (melts stone; creates lava hazard tile)

types.name: Aquas
types.description: The heavy, crushing weight of high-velocity water and hydraulic pressure.
types.interaction_logic: The only physical-Materium type. Deals base physical damage plus a drowning/concussive secondary. Most effective in coastal or swamp biomes. Also effective at extinguishing fire status.
types.status_trigger: Soaked (Tempest synergy active; -10% Physiology Hardening for 2 turns — waterlogged armor)
types.synergy: Tempest (Tempest-surge on soaked targets), Fire (extinguishes Ignited)

types.name: Athropium
types.description: Life force and nature damage. Attacks the very life force of all things. Common in witchcraft.
types.interaction_logic: Effective against all living things. Less effective against undead and constructs (diminished life force). Extreme forms can wither entire ecosystems. Oakpeople are paradoxically more resistant (life-force harmony).
types.status_trigger: Life-Drained (Vitality regeneration halved for 3 turns)
types.synergy: Temporal (combo accelerates target aging: Vigor -5 per hit)

types.name: Temporal
types.description: Time damage. Creates aging of the living and immaterial. Can instantly wither armor or kill via accelerated aging. Drains much of the caster's Materium Connection pool.
types.interaction_logic: Extremely high cost in Materium Connection. Bypasses most physical resistances (ages the target, not hits them). Can reduce item durability by 1 tier per hit. Cannot be regenerated from — Temporal damage to Vitality is permanent until a Soulkindler restores it.
types.status_trigger: Aged (Vigor -3 per hit permanently until healed by Soulkindler; items lose 1 durability tier)
types.resisted_by: Materium (Insulation, high-level only); The Stoic birth sign partially
types.special_note: Very rare. Casting costs 30% of Materium Connection pool per use.

description: Damage from decay, entropy, void, and domination. Targets Vitality and max HP. Common in necromancy, voidmagic, demonology, witchcraft, and dark warrior classes. Shunned in public and often illegal.

types.name: Darkvoid
types.description: The damaged energy of materium. Very unstable and difficult to control and direct.
types.interaction_logic: The base damage type of Shadow Arts, but unreliable. Has a ±20% damage variance on every cast (wild void energy). Deals bonus damage to Materium-attuned targets (their connection reacts to it). Purelight users are especially vulnerable.
types.status_trigger: Void-touched (Materium Connection pool -15% for 2 turns)
types.resisted_by: Shadow Connection (paradoxically reduces damage taken); Materium (Insulation) partially
types.special_note: Cannot be fully controlled even by masters. Always has variance.

types.name: Chill
types.description: Absolute, supernatural cold that saps Metabolism and slows physical movement to a crawl.
types.interaction_logic: Different from Climate-Cold. This is supernatural — not resisted by Weathering. Reduces Metabolism and Agilities. Deep synergy with high Conviction resistance slows the effect. The Eternal Winds natives only have partial resistance.
types.status_trigger: Slowed (Metabolism -20%; initiative lowered by 1 position for 2 turns)
types.synergy: Darkvoid (Frozen Void combo: target loses initiative for full turn)

types.name: Blight
types.description: Biological decay and rot targeting the Physiology stat. Mimics rapid aging. Creates lingering damage that hurts biology over time.
types.interaction_logic: Unique: Resisted by Physiology (Immunity) not magical resistance. Does not interact with Insulation. Lingering effect means even after combat the target continues taking damage. Can spread in area if conditions are right (wet biomes).
types.status_trigger: Blighted (Physiology -5 per turn for 3 turns; spreads to adjacent living targets on crit)
types.special_note: Swampbrood and Oakpeople are largely immune. The Boglands amplify Blight damage by 10%.

types.name: Darkblood
types.description: Attacks the blood of the target for damage or manipulation. Blood is strongly connected to the damaged energies of materium. Always hurts the wielder to a percentage.
types.interaction_logic: Caster takes 10% of damage dealt back as Darkblood damage (cannot be reduced). No resistance exists in most races. Stone Folk are unusually resilient. Can be used to control or puppet creatures with very low Vitality remaining.
types.status_trigger: Blood-Cursed (target cannot regenerate HP naturally for 3 turns; caster takes 10% backlash)
types.resisted_by: Stone Folk innate; Materium (Insulation, partial only)
types.special_note: Caster always takes 10% backlash damage regardless of resistances. Stone Folk are the rare exception to general vulnerability.

types.name: Arcane
types.description: Pure energy of the materium. Especially dangerous to unarmored targets — reacts and dwindles quickly against steel and iron.
types.interaction_logic: Deals significantly more damage to light or unarmored targets. Every tier of heavy metal armor reduces Arcane damage by 15% (max 60% reduction at plate). Ironically, the most dangerous to mages and scholars who wear no armor.
types.status_trigger: Arcane Burn (Materium Connection pool -10%; Cognition rolls -5% for 1 turn)
types.resisted_by: Heavy metal armor reduces significantly; Materium (Insulation)
types.special_note: Counter-intuitive: heavy warriors are best protected against Arcane. Mages are most vulnerable.

types.name: Darkmind
types.description: A damage type that attacks the weak of conviction, causing internal hemorrhage from within the mind.
types.interaction_logic: Damage scales inversely with Conviction — identical to Verdicium but from the opposite triad. More effective the lower the target's Conviction. Causes internal hemorrhage (Rupturing secondary on low-Conviction targets).
types.status_trigger: Mind-Ruptured (internal hemorrhage: Rupturing secondary damage if target Conviction below 30)
types.resisted_by: Conviction (all sub-traits); The Stoic birth sign immune
types.special_note: The Destroyer and Tyrant birth signs deal bonus Darkmind damage. The Stoic is completely immune.
